name: WP-secapi-input-validation
description: Add schema/bounds validation at the route + DTO boundary: zod .max() on quiz attempts and answer size (F-142); schema-parse the untrusted deep-link homeworkProblems JSON (F-158); .uuid() guard on the subjectId path param so malformed input is 4xx not an unhandled 500 (F-166); max-length on answerGiven before the O(m x n) Levenshtein routine (F-179); cap the dictation chunks/chunksWithPunctuation arrays (F-180).
acceptanceCriteria: F-142/158/166/179/180 each guarded with zod at the boundary; malformed/oversized-input tests return 4xx, never 500 or OOM.
bodyText:
Bundle brief — input-validation bounds
Rationale: five route/DTO boundaries accept unbounded or untrusted input, risking unhandled 500s, OOM/CPU-DoS (O(m×n) Levenshtein), and trust violations. Strategy: add zod schema/bounds validation at the boundary so malformed/oversized input is rejected as 4xx before reaching the handler. Scope: the five findings below; one PR; no behavior change for valid input.
F-142 — zod .max() on quiz attempts and answer size
F-158 — schema-parse the untrusted deep-link homeworkProblems JSON
F-166 — .uuid() guard on subjectId path param (malformed → 4xx not 500)
F-179 — max-length on answerGiven before the O(m×n) Levenshtein routine
F-180 — cap the dictation chunks / chunksWithPunctuation arrays
Acceptance Criteria: F-142/158/166/179/180 each guarded with zod at the boundary; malformed/oversized-input tests return 4xx, never 500 or OOM. Children are absorbed provenance (batches 707/708); the WP lands as one PR.
